items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2025-71389 - Calcom before 599 Remote Code Execution via RSC
id: 454255a5-51ee-52a3-b5d8-bcabd92ce201
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2025-71389 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-71389
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/24
modified: 2026/07/24
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2025-71389 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-24T01:50:43Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2025-71389", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2025-71389 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:7896026; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-24, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: High confidence based on: CVSS score 10.0 (Critical); EPSS score 99.560 (very high exploitation probability)
items.recommended_sla_action: EMERGENCY PATCH — Active exploitation confirmed or KEV listed
items.apex_ai_summary: [P1] Remote Code Execution - critical severity (Risk 9.0/10). [ALERT] CISA KEV CONFIRMED - actively exploited in the wild. Patch immediately. EPSS 99.56% exploitation probability (30-day forecast). MITRE ATT&CK mapped: T1059, T1190, T1210 (3 techniques). 23 indicators detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. Actor attribution: CDB-UNATTR-CVE. AI confidence: HIGH (81%) - strong data corroboration.
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-65604 - Skipper Incomplete Fix for CVE-2026-50197 Policy Bypass
id: 84aa24d6-89bf-52dc-9e9f-efb69b6b7ba3
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-65604 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: LOW | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-65604
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/24
modified: 2026/07/24
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: low
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-65604 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-24T01:50:43Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-65604", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc

items.title: pypdf: Possible infinite loop for not terminated inline images (ASCII85 and ASCIIHex filter)
items.description:
### Impact

An attacker who uses this vulnerability can craft a PDF which leads to an infinite loop. This requires parsing the content stream of a page with a not terminated inline image, as done when extracting the page text for example. It only affects the ASCII85 and ASCIIHex filters.

### Patches

This has been fixed in [pypdf==6.14.2](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/releases/tag/6.14.2).

### Workarounds

If you cannot upgrade yet, consider applying the changes from PR [#3892](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/pull/3892).
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window
items.apex_ai_summary: [HIGH] Vulnerability advisory. Risk score 7.6/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.

items.title: pypdf: Possible long runtimes for repeated malformed cross-reference entries
items.description:
### Impact

An attacker who uses this vulnerability can craft a PDF which leads to long runtimes. This requires cross-reference streams with repeated malformed cross-reference streams.

### Patches

This has been fixed in [pypdf==6.14.0](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/releases/tag/6.14.0).

### Workarounds

If you cannot upgrade yet, consider applying the changes from PR [#3887](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/pull/3887).

items.title: pypdf: Possible large memory usage for wrong image dimensions
items.description:
### Impact

An attacker who uses this vulnerability can craft a PDF which leads to large memory usage. This requires loading images where the declared size values are much too large compared to the actual data.

### Patches

This has been fixed in [pypdf==6.14.0](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/releases/tag/6.14.0).

### Workarounds

If you cannot upgrade yet, consider applying the changes from PR [#3888](https://github.com/py-pdf/pypdf/pull/3888).

items.title: c3p0 can, in combination with other libraries, compose to a "sink" for deserialization gadgets
items.description:
### Impact

The JDBC spec defines the interface `DataSource`, with a method called `getConnection()`, and `ConnectionPoolDataSource`, with a method called `getPooledConnection()`. These methods are potentially dangerous. One way or another they trigger calls into JDBC drivers, which themselves are complicated, flexible tools which may be, and in practice sometimes have proven to be, susceptible to abuse.

Unfortunately, the JavaBean framework treats methods of this form, `getXXX()`, as JavaBean "properties", which JavaBean-related libraries often presume represent quick, safe-to-look-up, state or configuration.

Attackers therefore can construct malicious `DataSource` objects — objects whose calls to `getConnection()` or `getPooledConnection()` would trigger attacks via vulnerable JDBC drivers — and bundle them in contexts that automatically look up bean properties on deserialization. If an attacker can smuggle such an object in serialized form to a location from which an application w
